pi_items.source: Web verification - both sites confirmed as real, publicly accessible websites; GitHub brand toolkit at brand.github.com and Python Software Foundation official site

BrandColors and multiple design palette sources confirm GitHub has 8-9 brand colors (#4078c0, #6cc644, #bd2c00, #c9510c, #6e5494, #fafafa, #f5f5f5, #333) and Python has 5+ colors (#306998, #FFD43B, #4B8BBE, #FFE873, #646464). Both sites exceed the minimum threshold of 6.

GitHub uses system font stack: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Both target websites use multiple font families. Threshold of 2 is realistic and verifiable.
Standard web design practice uses minimum of 3 font sizes (headers, body text, small text/captions). This is a reasonable and verifiable threshold for any modern website.

DISCREPANCY FOUND: Task.json metadata specifies 'min_file_size_bytes': 500, but verifier.py line 128 checks 'if report.get("size_bytes", 0) > 800'. The verifier uses 800 bytes as the actual threshold for full credit (10 pts), not 500 as stated in metadata.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 24 and export_result.sh line 21. Both scripts reference this exact path.

privileged_info_summary: GitHub and Python.org are real, publicly accessible websites with documented brand color palettes (GitHub: 8-9 colors including #4078c0, #6cc644, #bd2c00; Python: 5+ colors including #306998, #FFD43B). Both sites use multiple font families and sizes. The task requires extracting actual design data from these sites using Microsoft Edge DevTools. The minimum thresholds (6 hex codes, 2 font families, 3 font sizes) are realistic and verifiable. A critical discrepancy exists: metadata specifies min_file_size_bytes as 500, but verifier.py checks for >800 bytes.
